message:
feat(core,abi): port ns_name_ntop/pton DNS wire-format codec to core::resolv::dns_name (bd-nsn-epic)

Lifts ~392 lines of inline RFC 1035 DNS-name wire ↔ presentation
conversion across 4 abi entry points (ns_name_ntop, ns_name_pton,
__ns_name_ntop, __ns_name_pton) into a pure-safe
core::resolv::dns_name module.

New core surface:
  pub const NS_MAXLABEL = 63
  pub const NS_MAXCDNAME = 255
  pub const NS_MAXDNAME = 1025
  pub enum NameError {
      OutputTooSmall, InvalidLabel, EmptyLabelInMiddle,
      InvalidEscape, CompressionPointer,
  }
  pub fn name_ntop(wire: &[u8], dst: &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize, NameError>
  pub fn name_pton(text: &[u8], dst: &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize, NameError>

Both preserve abi byte-for-byte semantics: high-bits-set length byte
rejected as CompressionPointer; `\DDD` octal escapes for non-printable
bytes (outside 0x20..0x7F); literal `\.` and `\\` escapes; root-only
input encodes as a single zero byte; empty-middle-label rejected.

abi changes (in glibc_internal_abi.rs):
  - 4 inline impls (~392 lines combined) replaced with thin shims.
  - Notable BSD-vs-public-semantic difference preserved:
    __ns_name_pton returns 1 when the input ends with `.` (fully
    qualified), 0 otherwise, and -1 on error — distinct from the
    public ns_name_pton which returns the byte count. The abi shim
    detects the trailing dot to compute the BSD return value.

Verified via rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc_bd-nsn-2):
  - 22 new core::resolv::dns_name tests pass (incl. 2 round-trip
    property tests covering both simple and escaped names)
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass
  - 29/29 ns_name integration tests pass (incl. the BSD-semantic
    fully-qualified test, encode-to-wire test, root-domain test,
    null-args test, ntop_decodes_wire test, ntop_pton roundtrip)
  - cargo clippy -p core -p abi -D warnings clean
  - cargo fmt --check clean

Closes bd-nsn-epic.
